message:
fix(http): whitespace Accept-Encoding normalization, WebSocket trailing byte handling, compress encoding filter

http/compress.rs: negotiate_encoding now trims whitespace from the
Accept-Encoding header before checking for empty. A whitespace-only
header like "   " was not matching the empty-header branch, causing
gzip-only configurations to return None (no acceptable encoding)
instead of falling through to the identity/first-supported default.
Test added: negotiate_whitespace_only_accept_encoding_matches_empty_header.

websocket/handshake.rs: HttpRequest::parse_with_trailing splits on the
first \r\n\r\n (or \n\n) boundary and returns both the parsed request
and any trailing bytes. This handles the case where a client pipelines
WebSocket frames immediately after the upgrade request in the same TCP
segment. The existing parse() method delegates to parse_with_trailing
and discards the trailing bytes for backward compatibility.

websocket/server.rs: WebSocketAcceptor::accept now passes trailing
bytes from parse_with_trailing into ServerWebSocket::from_upgraded,
which seeds them into the read buffer. Previously, pipelined frames
that arrived in the same TCP read as the handshake would be silently
lost, causing the first recv() after upgrade to hang until the client
sent another frame. The accept_from_request path passes &[] since
the request was already parsed externally.

web/compress.rs: content_encoding_available changed from &ContentEncoding
to ContentEncoding (Copy type) to avoid a closure reference lifetime
issue, and the filter closure adapted accordingly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
